summary: Self-correcting agent framework that unifies failure detection, classification, and recovery; demonstrated 80% reduction in mean-time-to-recovery when combined with OpenClaw multi-agent orchestration.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — directly addresses AIBlocked dead-end; retry wrapper on ProcessRunner is architecturally clean

summary: Multi-agent orchestration system with structured failure recovery showing 80% MTTR reduction in production; pairs with PALADIN for unified failure handling.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — production evidence supporting HID-120 retry pattern

summary: Survey formalizing instance-specific token budget allocation policies for LLMs; proposes adaptive model tier selection based on remaining budget, covering a 4,500x cost spread from economy to frontier models.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — policyEngine.ts is natural integration point; hardcoded model mapping is a known gap

source: RL for Multi-Agent Systems through Orchestration Traces (arxiv 2605.02801)
summary: May 2026 paper introducing a minimal JSON schema for replayable multi-agent orchestration traces, enabling post-run attribution analysis and RL-based policy optimization of agent coordination.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — AgentForge's deterministic state machine maps cleanly to the trace schema; prerequisite for attribution and future RL loop

source: Speculative Actions: Lossless Framework for Faster Agentic Systems (arxiv 2510.04371)
summary: October 2025 paper proposing speculative execution for agents — predict likely next actions with faster models while slower executors catch up; shows 1.8x-3.7x wall-clock speedups and up to 6x cost reduction.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — addresses parallel agent execution gap; however requires non-trivial ProcessRunner refactor for subprocess parallelism; deferred

source: SWE-RL: Self-Improving Agents via Self-Play (Meta, Dec 2025)
summary: Meta's Dec 2025 agent alternates between bug-injector and solver roles on real codebases to generate training data; enables agents to improve without external labeling.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — reflection-based plan repair is relevant to the plan revision cycle; scored 4.05 but lost to higher-impact candidates

summary: Andrej Karpathy's April 2026 pattern treating raw conversation logs as source code that an LLM 'compiles' into structured knowledge wikis; enables agents to maintain continuously improving knowledge bases.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — addresses no-agent-memory-across-runs gap; requires DB schema changes and memory management layer; deferred due to complexity

summary: Google's open standard for heterogeneous agent coordination using HTTP/SSE/JSON-RPC; Agent Cards advertise capabilities; 150+ organizations adopted as of 2026 including Microsoft, AWS, Salesforce.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — relevant for future federation of AgentForge with external agents; not an immediate priority since system is single-tenant

summary: Zep's Graphiti introduces bitemporal edges for knowledge graphs that handle contradictory facts over time; enables agents to reason about when facts were true, not just what is currently true.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — interesting for persistent agent memory but too architecturally distant from AgentForge's current model

summary: Canonical survey on LLM-powered autonomous agents covering memory, planning, tool use, and self-criticism patterns; updated through 2025-2026 with long-horizon and task-decoupled planning.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — foundational reference; AgentForge already implements most surveyed patterns; no novel gaps surfaced

source: Jim Fan / NVIDIA Voyager Lifelong Learning Agent
summary: LLM-powered lifelong learning agent in Minecraft that self-improves in-context through code generation and self-reflection; 3.3x more items, 15.3x faster milestone achievement than baselines.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — embodied/game AI paradigm; continuous skill acquisition doesn't translate directly to CLI subprocess orchestration

summary: New TypeScript-native multi-agent orchestration framework launched April 2026 that converts goals to task DAGs automatically; known users include temodar-agent (~60 stars).
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — competitor/reference architecture; DAG-based task decomposition approach worth monitoring but doesn't suggest a discrete enhancement

summary: Enterprise case studies on AI token cost runaway, including Uber burning 2026 token budget by April after Claude Code rollout; documents three-tier safeguard patterns for production deployments.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — production evidence supporting HID-121 token budget feature; referenced as secondary source in that issue
